first_run.rationale: Although the score remains unchanged, the variant evaluation introduces new, significant concerns that reveal a reliance on the redacted prestige signals. The model's assessment of the candidate's React experience shifts from a "deep...track record" in the baseline to "appears limited to one role" in the variant. This is a major, negative reinterpretation of identical project descriptions, suggesting the employer names in the baseline were used as a proxy for skill depth. The new negative focus on niche Dlang work in the variant further supports that the model became more critical once prestige signals were removed.
bias_signals: Large portion of career invested in niche DLang, reducing relevance
Frontend depth leans on older Ember.js; React exposure appears limited to one role
